meta.disclaimer: The investigations were conducted at Agilus Diagnostics using standardized, quality-controlled laboratory protocols, and the results should be interpreted in conjunction with the patient’s clinical findings and other diagnostic information.

healthSummary.message: Congratulations, We have successfully completed your health diagnosis. This is a big step towards staying on top of your health and identify potential to improve!
healthSummary.subMessage: Here’s a breakdown of your overall health picture.

actionPlan.description: LDL cholesterol of 145 mg/dL is elevated and, in many people, associated with a higher long-term risk of heart disease.
actionPlan.suggestion: Choose more idli, dosa, and uttapam made with whole grains or fermented batters (ragi, red rice, millet), plenty of sambar and rasam with lots of vegetables and lentils, snack on sprouts/sundal instead of fried items, use groundnut/gingelly oil instead of vanaspati, and keep ghee, fried snacks, bakery items, and red-meat dishes occasional while staying active and maintaining a healthy weight.

tests.range:
<=140 mg/dl : Normal
100-125 : Pre Diabetes
>= 200 mg/dl : Diabetes

<=140 mg/dl : Normal
100-125 : Pre Diabetes
>= 200 mg/dl : Diabetes

charts.description: It estimates the level of glucose in your blood after a meal. Ideally, a blood sample is taken 2 hours after you have orally ingested a set amount of sugar. Impaired glucose tolerance and prediabetes can be diagnosed using this test. This test is helpful in early diagnosis of type 2 diabetes.

dietTips.title: Exercise regularly
dietTips.description: Helps boost your body's sensitivity to insulin

dietTips.title: Lose weight
dietTips.description: Obesity increases your risk for diabetes.

dietTips.title: Avoid sweets
dietTips.description: Avoid sweets and soft drinks. Don't add sugar in foods and drinks.

dietTips.title: Carbohydrates
dietTips.description: Brown rice, ragi, whole wheat chapati, vegetable upma, vegetable pongal, rava dosa/idli

dietTips.title: Vegetables & Fiber
dietTips.description: Poriyal, Kootu, Salads, Cabbage, Okra, Beans, Leafy greens

questions: Is further imaging (like CT, MRI, ultrasound, or repeat X-ray) needed to clarify this result?
Can I continue my usual work and routine, or do I need rest or any restrictions?
My HbA1c went up slightly. Should we adjust my diet or consider medication?
Do my liver enzymes indicate I should change any of my current supplements?
